description: Discover Agiasos, a picturesque village in Lesbos, Greece. Learn about its history, culture, and how to get there.

village.title: The Village of Agiasos Lesbos
village.description: Discover the picturesque village of Agiasos in Lesbos, Greece.
village.text: Agiasos is a picturesque village in the heart of Lesbos, Greece, known for its traditional architecture, rich cultural heritage, and beautiful natural surroundings. Nestled in the shadow of Mount Olympos, the village has been a centre of art, religion, and craftsmanship for centuries.

talkingTiles.title: The Talking Tiles
talkingTiles.description: An autonomous bilingual guide for Agiasos with QR codes on traditional ceramic tiles.
talkingTiles.intro: «The talking tiles» is a unique, autonomous guide where new technologies are intertwined with traditional elements, presenting an interesting autonomous tour of the village of Agiasos.
talkingTiles.aimText: The aim of this action is the creation and placement of handmade ceramic tiles in historical buildings and points of interest in Agiasos, hand-painted with unique traditional designs inspired by the nature of the Lesvian countryside but also with patterns from loom designs unique in Lesvos, creation of local painters and potters of Agiasos with generations of experience.
talkingTiles.howText1: At the bottom of the tile we placed QR Codes and through the camera of our mobile or QR Code Scanner application, we scan the tile and we are redirected to the website of Visitagiasos.gr in the respective online bilingual guide in Greek and English with audiovisual material for the specific point.
talkingTiles.howText2: There, apart from the detailed explanation of the point of interest in Agiasos we can also find mapped routes for the proposed alternative paths to the next tile, thus organizing our tour according to our interests, learning through technology for the historical sites, museums or even businesses around the point of interest, «marrying» the local culture with technology and creating a useful and timeless tool, which with a simple movement of our mobile phone will fill us with knowledge and valid information.
talkingTiles.a1: Installed over 20 handmade ceramic tiles across Agiasos
talkingTiles.a2: Created a complete bilingual digital guide (Greek & English)
talkingTiles.a3: Mapped out multiple walking routes connecting points of interest
talkingTiles.a4: Partnered with local artisans for authentic tile production
talkingTiles.a5: Integrated QR technology with traditional craftsmanship
talkingTiles.a6: Received recognition from cultural preservation organizations
talkingTiles.resultsText: The 26 «Talking Tiles» of Visit Agiasos, scattered in the village of Agiasos since February 2020 helped our visitors in many ways. Here are some of the ways we helped our travelers.
talkingTiles.result1Text: Provided easy access to information about the village of Agiasos and its history, culture and traditions.
talkingTiles.result2Text: Helped visitors walk to places they would not otherwise have discovered and explore the village of Agiasos in a unique and interesting way.
talkingTiles.result3Text: Managed to shed light on the unique traditional motifs of Agiasos. And helped visitors see the village from a different perspective.
talkingTiles.result4Text: Attracted thousands of travelers and introduced them to a beautiful and historic village.
talkingTiles.result5Text: Created an interesting, family friendly and special «treasure» hunt experience for visitors of the village.
talkingTiles.mapText: Click on the image below to find out where the talking tiles are located in the village of Agiasos and begin your treasure hunt!

directions.title: How to Get to Agiasos
directions.description: Directions and travel information for getting to Agiasos, Lesbos.
directions.text: Agiasos is located in the central-south part of Lesbos, approximately 28km from Mytilene, the capital of the island. The village is nestled in a verdant valley on the northern slopes of Mount Olympos at an altitude of about 475 meters.
directions.byCarText: From Mytilene, take the road towards Agiasos (approximately 30 minutes drive). The road is well-maintained and offers scenic views of the island's landscape.
directions.byBusText: KTEL buses operate regular routes from Mytilene to Agiasos. Check the local bus schedule for current timetables.
directions.byFerryText: Lesbos is accessible by ferry from Piraeus and other Aegean islands, or by plane to Odysseas Elytis Airport (MJT) in Mytilene.
